title: URIs, URL Resolution, Origins, DNS, and Destination Identity · Go Client Systems · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A platform SDK team integrating a staging control plane must build a first protocol model from narrated fixtures and explain each policy boundary for uris, url resolution, origins, dns, and destination identity, producing destination-resolution and origin contract. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a Go 1.26 net/http destination-resolution and origin contract while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case prac…
objectives: Parse scheme, authority, host, port, path, query, and fragment and resolve relative references against the correct base. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Encode path segments and query names or values at the correct structural layer without double encoding. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Compare origins from scheme, host, and effective port while separating URI authority, Host, and connection address. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Uri Components Resolution
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Go staging control-plane client receives base https://api2.example.test/v1/items/ and reference ../reports?q=a%2Fb. Resolve it structurally, compare origin and authority, trace two DNS answers, and reject any connected destination outside the approved range. Competency probe: Parse scheme, authority, host, port, path, query, and fragment and resolve relative references against the correct base. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Uri Components Resolution.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for parse scheme, authority, host, port, path, query, and fragment and resolve relative references against the correct base. demonstrate the result through go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, roundtripper, and httptest evidence. For Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Uri Components Resolution, keep Uri Components Resolution connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.text: Go staging control-plane client receives base https://api2.example.test/v1/items/ and reference ../reports?q=a%2Fb. Resolve it structurally, compare origin and authority, trace two DNS answers, and reject any connected destination outside the approved range. Competency probe: Parse scheme, authority, host, port, path, query, and fragment and resolve relative references against the correct base. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

steps.title: Go Audit the initial evidence packet · Workshop Destination Validation
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Go staging control-plane client receives base https://api3.example.test/v1/items/ and reference ../reports?q=a%2Fb. Resolve it structurally, compare origin and authority, trace two DNS answers, and reject any connected destination outside the approved range. Competency probe: Canonicalize and validate schemes, hosts, IP literals, ports, IDNs, and resolved destinations before granting network authority. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
